name: Lou Rawls
abstract: Louis Allen Rawls (December 1, 1933 – January 6, 2006) was an American baritone singer. He released 61 albums, sold more than 40 million records, and had numerous charting singles, most notably the song "You'll Never Find Another Love Like Mine".

concerts.label: Jimmy Smith, Oscar Peterson Trio, and Lou Rawls with the Frankie Dunlop Trio
Jazz Concert featuring the Dave Brubeck Quartet, Jimmy Smith and His Organ Trio, and Lou Rawls

works.t: Garota de Ipanema (The Girl from Ipanema)
Living Double in a World of Trouble

Blue Note Records and WBGO Benefit Jazz Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Chazz Frichtel
about_quotes.evidence: Reg Powell, and a drummer, Jack Toker, lives in Seattle. And Reg was kind of putting this thing together for Rebop for Island Records or something... Reg Powell... was a guy that was Lou Rawls’ music director
about_quotes.doc_title: Chazz Frichtel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 13, 2016

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I met a guy named Kenny Elliott, who is now with Lou Rawls, has been with Lou Rawls for I don’t know, for a long time
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Caramanica
about_quotes.evidence: I’d been playing with Lou and I knew the kind of stuff he liked to hear
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Caramanica interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, July 27, 2011

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Lou and I have always had – we always had a good rapport, good relationship, especially doing UNCF year after year after year after year after year after year.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: She says, “Well, in the singers you have Koko Taylor, Gary U.S. Bonds, Isaac Hayes and Lou Rawls... I was really glad to be a part of that.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: I remember Lou Rawls was interviewed by somebody, and he said, “All I know, man, they used to say that I was a rhythm-and-blues singer, then a soul singer, then a pop singer.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
